title: Java Concurrency in Practice Workshop - Chapter-based workshop with 10 content slides per chapter

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 1?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about introduction.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Introduction chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Introduction.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Introduction chapter?
options: Name the problem concurrency is solving
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Introduction chapter?
options: Adding threads before identifying a bottleneck
Notice where asynchronous work becomes simpler or riskier
Use the later chapters as a map for the failure modes introduced here
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 2?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about thread safety.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Thread Safety chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Thread Safety.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Thread Safety chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Thread Safety chapter?
options: Identify who may touch it concurrently
Protect compound actions as one unit
Prefer simpler designs such as statelessness when possible
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 3?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about sharing objects.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Sharing Objects chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Sharing Objects.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Sharing Objects chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Sharing Objects chapter?
options: Publishing objects before construction is complete
Treat visibility rules as first-class correctness rules
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 4?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about composing objects.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Composing Objects chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Composing Objects.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Composing Objects chapter?
options: Name the invariants that span fields
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Composing Objects chapter?
options: Assuming thread-safe fields imply a thread-safe class
Decide whether safety is delegated or owned locally
Document the lock and the state it guards
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 5?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about building blocks.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Building Blocks chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Building Blocks.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Building Blocks chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Building Blocks chapter?
options: Relying on synchronized wrappers for compound workflows
Use concurrent collections where shared access is hot
Let abstractions carry synchronization policy when they fit the problem
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 6?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about task execution.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Task Execution chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Task Execution.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Task Execution chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Task Execution chapter?
options: Embedding execution policy inside business logic
Separate task logic from execution policy
Look for parallelism only after independence is clear
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 7?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about cancellation and shutdown.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Cancellation and Shutdown chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Cancellation and Shutdown.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Cancellation and Shutdown chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Cancellation and Shutdown chapter?
options: Design services with explicit lifecycle methods
Restore or propagate interrupt status correctly
Test how cancellation behaves under partial progress
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 8?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about applying thread pools.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Applying Thread Pools chapter?
options: Implicit Couplings Between Tasks and Execution Policies
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Applying Thread Pools.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Applying Thread Pools chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Applying Thread Pools chapter?
options: Copying pool sizes from other systems
Watch for blocked or dependent tasks
Choose queues and rejection policies intentionally
Use executor extension points for visibility and control
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 9?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about gui applications.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the GUI Applications chapter?

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the GUI Applications chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the GUI Applications chapter?
options: Running long work on the event thread
Push heavy work to background execution
Treat responsiveness budgets as a real design constraint
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 10?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about avoiding liveness hazards.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Avoiding Liveness Hazards chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Avoiding Liveness Hazards.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Avoiding Liveness Hazards chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Avoiding Liveness Hazards chapter?

Design retries with timeouts, randomness, or backoff when needed
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Performance and Scalability.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Performance and Scalability chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Performance and Scalability chapter?

Tune only after correctness and liveness are stable

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 12?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about testing concurrent programs.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Testing Concurrent Programs chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Testing Concurrent Programs.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Testing Concurrent Programs chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Testing Concurrent Programs chapter?
options: Assuming a passing unit test proves concurrency safety
Design code so concurrency rules are observable in tests

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 13?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about explicit locks.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Explicit Locks chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Explicit Locks.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Explicit Locks chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Explicit Locks chapter?
options: Using explicit locks without a clear need
Pair every acquisition with explicit release discipline
Use advanced features only when they solve a real problem
Measure before claiming a lock strategy is better
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 14?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about building custom synchronizers.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Building Custom Synchronizers chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Building Custom Synchronizers.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Building Custom Synchronizers chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Building Custom Synchronizers chapter?
options: Using wait or condition queues without a clear predicate
Bind conditions to the right lock and state
Prefer existing utilities when they fit
Use AQS only with a clean acquisition and release model
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

modules.name: Chapter 15: Atomic Variables and Non-blocking Synchronization

questions.question: What is the main concern of Chapter 15?
questions.explanation: This chapter is specifically about atomic variables and non-blocking synchronization.

questions.question: Which section belongs to the Atomic Variables and Non-blocking Synchronization chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter manifest lists this topic under Atomic Variables and Non-blocking Synchronization.

questions.question: Which review habit best matches the Atomic Variables and Non-blocking Synchronization chapter?
questions.explanation: The checklist slide turns the chapter into a practical review lens.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the Atomic Variables and Non-blocking Synchronization chapter?
options: Replacing locks with atomics without checking invariant scope
Keep retry loops bounded and measurable
Compare lock-based and non-blocking behavior under real contention
Choose the simplest primitive that preserves the invariant
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.

questions.question: Which trap is explicitly called out for the The Java Memory Model chapter?
options: Assuming source-code order is what other threads observe
Fall back to higher-level patterns when low-level reasoning gets fragile
questions.explanation: The Common Traps slide highlights recurring mistakes tied to this chapter's topic.
